source_paragraph: company goals by proposing a novel recommendation system for internal business processes, grounded in the COBIT 2019 framework. The system employs graph algorithms to map relationships, semantic similarity measure- ments to align enterprise goals with management objectives, and LSTM combined with TF-IDF methods to analyze and recommend specific activities. Experimental results show that this system optimizes IT management practices, enhances operational efficiency, and ensures regulatory compliance. Additionally, a user-friendly graphical representation of internal business pro- cesses using Neo4j enables stakeholders to visualize complex relationships within the COBIT framework, facilitating informed decision-making. This research advances IT governance practices by providing organizations with a valuable tool to automate and improve IT strategies, effectively achieving business objectives. source_paragraph: PREVIOUS WORKS Among the relevant published studies in the log-based anomaly detection space, sequence-based is one of the most commonly used approaches. Therefore, there are limited relevant references as this study employs a point-based approach. Below are the relevant baselines from previous research: 1) Pylogsentiment is the first study which employs sentiment analysis-based anomaly detection on operat- ing system logs using GRU and GloVe embedding. 2) SentiLog , similar to , use a two-layered BiLSTM and GloVe embedding model to perform anomaly detection on parallel file system logs. 3) TransSentLog is a further development of and uses a two-layered transformer encoder which used two attention heads and GloVe embedding along with integrated gradients to add explainability to the trained model.

source_paragraph: I. INTRODUCTION I. INTRODUCTION The use of drones has become increasingly common in various applications, including aerial photography, surveying, and monitoring. However, drones can also be involved in incidents such as accidents, violations, or even crimes, making it necessary to investigate drone logs as part of forensic investigations. In a forensic investigation, drone logs are essential sources of information that can help investigators reconstruct the events leading up to an incident. Drone logs typically include information such as flight paths, altitude, speed, battery life, sensor data, and other telemetry information . source_paragraph: presented a novel multi-head attention mechanism that uses various atten- tion functions to capture diverse contextual elements. The multi-head attention approach allows attention to be simul- taneously allocated to information from different subspaces and representational positions. In contrast to soft attention, which focuses attention softly on the entire input to produce a context representation with weights determined based on the relevance of each part of the input, soft attention relies on only one attention mechanism. Kumar et al. developed the multi-head attention- based BiLSTM (MHA-BiLSTM) model. The results are quite promising compared to the use of single-head attention. How- ever, using the BiLSTM model is computationally expensive, especially when dealing with large datasets . BiLSTM is also prone to overfitting,

In feature extraction, a daily confidence score from the classification model is used to quantify sentiment. The active learning model produced a monthly classification model to represent model knowledge. The Transformer Encoder Gated Recurrent Unit (TEGRU) out- performed another Transformer architecture for stock price forecasting on eight stock issuers. The results of the EEG trial recordings have been mea- sured using the Self-Assessment Manikin (SAM). SAM has four emotional dimensions, namely valence, arousal, domi- nance, and liking, with ratings 0-9. For valence and arousal classes, emotion labels are divided into two categories: low and high. CONCLUSION This research demonstrates that the YOLOv8 model, specifically the YOLOv8m variant, provides excellent results in detecting Peritoneal Carcinomatosis (PC) from the used laparoscopic image dataset. By utilizing and fine-tuning the pre-trained YOLOv8 weights, this model shows superior performance with a mean Average Precision (mAP50) of 0.871, precision of 0.893, recall of 0.785, and an F1 score of 0.84. The predicted images also show that the performance of YOLOv8 with pre-trained weights is able to predict images well. All YOLOv8 scales are accurate in making predictions, with no errors in image prediction. In contrast, without using pre-trained weights, YOLOv8n and YOLOv8s still make errors in prediction, with some lesions being missed.
